body.title: agent_datacenter: blocked Postgres returns clear error to dependents immediately
body.result: DeviceBlockedError in agent_datacenter/skeleton/exceptions.py. PostgresDevice._check_not_blocked() raises it instantly (<0.1ms) before any DB op. block_type='manual'|'auto'. RegistrationError also defined there.
body.description:
When Postgres is blocked and a dependent device tries to query it, it gets an immediate clear error — not a timeout.

Problem: Without this, a Postgres block during DB migration causes dependent devices to hang for their TCP timeout (30s+). Clear error enables fast failover.

Proposed shape:
- In PostgresDevice: before any DB operation, check registry for own block status
  If block_type is 'manual' or 'auto': immediately raise DeviceBlockedError({'device': 'postgres', 'block_type': ...})
  Error shape: {'error': 'device_blocked', 'device_id': 'postgres', 'block_type': 'manual'|'auto', 'blocked_since': ISO8601}
- Dependent devices that catch DeviceBlockedError can surface it immediately without waiting for TCP timeout
- Test with: Igor device calling PostgresDevice.query() while Postgres is manually blocked

Affected files:
- ~/dev/src/agent_datacenter/agent_datacenter/devices/postgres/device.py (pre-op block check)
- ~/dev/src/agent_datacenter/agent_datacenter/skeleton/exceptions.py (DeviceBlockedError)

Design rules:
- docs-in-code: comment explaining the motivation (migration safety)

Scope boundary:
IN: pre-op block check, DeviceBlockedError exception with error shape
OUT: dependent device error handling (each device handles DeviceBlockedError in its own code)

Test plan:
- Manually block postgres in registry; PostgresDevice.health() raises DeviceBlockedError
- DeviceBlockedError has block_type='manual'
- DeviceBlockedError raised immediately (< 100ms), not after TCP timeout
